texto: If I have learned one thing in the years of my existence, one nugget of wisdom from having lived in the midst of disputations over faith and the nature of the world, it is that everything ends. This is both the blessing and the punishment of God upon the foolish tribe that calls itself man. We can embrace the end or we can weep, but the ghost of time closes all doors with a finality that can never be gainsaid.
explicacao: Citação de Kamran Pasha sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “If I have learned one thing in the years of my existence, one nugget of wisdom from having lived …” articula reflexao, emptiness, emptiness-sacrifice em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e termino.
